operating_state: Prologis generated $8.2 billion in rental revenues for 2025, up from $7.5 billion, driven by favorable mark-to-market on lease rollovers at 50.1% net effective rent change and strong occupancy at 95.6%. Core FFO increased to $5.56 billion from $5.31 billion, while net earnings attributable to common stockholders declined to $3.32 billion from $3.73 billion due to lower gains on real estate dispositions. Real Estate Segment NOI increased reflecting embedded rent growth from accumulated market rent increases despite recent quarters of lower market rental growth.
cost_structure: Rental expenses increased to $1.96 billion from $1.77 billion, while general and administrative expenses rose due to inflationary increases and higher compensation expenses including workforce additions. Real estate depreciation and amortization increased to $2.54 billion from $2.50 billion, reflecting continued asset base expansion. Strategic Capital segment NOI declined as promote revenue fell significantly from prior year levels.
material_events: Prologis generated $2.7 billion in net proceeds from property dispositions and realized $944 million in net gains, principally from contributions to unconsolidated co-investment ventures and third-party sales. The company listed China AMC Prologis Logistics REIT on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange in December, retaining 20.7% ownership. Prologis issued $3.4 billion of senior notes with 4.2% weighted average interest rate and completed debt issuance while amending credit facility extending maturity to June 2029.
macro_exposures: The company experienced economic disruption related to tariff policy proposals announced in April 2025, though performance strengthened as the year progressed. Foreign currency exposure remains significant with 56.1% of debt denominated in non-USD currencies. Foreign currency and derivative exposures shifted to net losses of $125 million from net gains of $68 million, indicating increased volatility in international operations.
forward_outlook: Development activity focused on build-to-suit projects representing 60.9% of new consolidated development starts, with the development pipeline expected to complete before September 2027 at $5.1 billion total estimated investment. The company maintains $7.6 billion in total available liquidity. Management expects continued long-term revenue growth supported by embedded rent growth in the in-place portfolio and development pipeline.
uncertainty_profile: The potential impact of ongoing economic uncertainty on future financial condition and operating results remains difficult to predict according to management. The company faces $1.9 billion in debt maturities during 2026 requiring refinancing or repayment, while unconsolidated co-investment ventures carry $19.7 billion in third-party non-recourse debt. Standard debt covenant restrictions apply on additional indebtedness and asset dispositions.
